definitions: Each of four parts of a circle, plane, or sphere divided by two lines or planes at right angles.
Each of four sections of the abdomen, used as a reference point for describing the location of pain or abnormalities.
A historical instrument used in astronomy and navigation for measuring angles, typically a quarter of a circle with a graduated scale.
examples: The city was divided into four quadrants.
Pain was localized to the upper right quadrant.
Ancient mariners used quadrants to determine their latitude.
